mnemonics: SLAM (State, Latches/Flip-Flops, Always Clock, Memory) for core components.
SET RESET (SR) Latch: Set and Reset are the two inputs.
JK Flip-Flop: Jump and Kill, the 'King' of flip-flops because it has no invalid state.
keyPoints: Sequential circuits have memory; their output depends on both current inputs and previous states.
The fundamental building block is a bistable element, most commonly a flip-flop (e.g., D, JK, T).
Synchronous circuits use a clock signal to coordinate changes of state, preventing instability.
commonMistakes: Confusing latches (level-triggered) with flip-flops (edge-triggered). Latches are transparent while the enable is active; flip-flops only change on a clock edge.
Forgetting to initialize the state of a sequential circuit, leading to unpredictable startup behavior (e.g., metastability).
Incorrectly drawing or interpreting state diagrams or tables, misrepresenting the next state logic.
